promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
wildcard_host_permissions: NONE — AG-363 removed https://*/* from manifest.chrome.json and manifest.firefox.json (unchanged by this promotion)
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-378 and AG-PROMPT-379 (PDF Author metadata precedence + field-blind metadata confidential fixes). Exactly two allow-listed files changed since the AG-377 promotion lineage (source commit aa69a52): src/detection/packs/global.ts and src/detection/metadataSignals.ts (confirmed via git diff against aa69a52, restricted to the full AG-269 allow-list, and independently via a full recursive src/ tree diff between dev and release before this promotion — no other allow-listed file touched; public/manifest.chrome.json and public/manifest.firefox.json confirmed byte-identical between dev and release, no permission drift). AG-378 root cause: global-dob (global.ts) only matched numeric-format dates (DD-MM-YYYY etc.), not spelled-out month names (e.g. '22 April 2010') — for that format no content PII signal fired at all, leaving a low-severity meta.author metadata signal as the only signal, which trivially 'won' the explanation, looking like metadata precedence when it was actually a content-detection gap. Separately, meta.author (metadataSignals.ts) fired on any non-blocklisted Author value, including bare abbreviations ('AC') and tool auto-fill names ('Microsoft Word'). Fixed by extending global-dob's pattern to also match spelled-out English month-name dates, and by requiring meta.author values to have 2+ tokens and excluding known document-tool names. AG-379 root cause (FIELD_BLIND_METADATA_CONFIDENTIAL): analyzeMetadataForRisks (metadataSignals.ts) blended author/lastModifiedBy/creator/producer together with title/subject/company/manager/description/category/keywords into one keyword-searchable string, so a keyword in any of those four PROVENANCE fields (commonly auto-filled by tools, not deliberately written) could trigger critical-severity meta.confidential/meta.ma_deal and other checks with no relation to actual content. Fixed by excluding author/lastModifiedBy/creator/producer from the keyword blend; title/subject/company/manager/description/category/keywords remain eligible (a Title of 'Confidential Q4 Finance Review' remains a genuine, intended signal). A self-caught regression (an early-return that would have also silently broken the unrelated meta.author check once author left the blend) was found and fixed within the same AG-379 run, before being reported. Both prompts are detection-pattern/validation precision fixes only — no detection threshold structure, RiskSignal structure, deduplication, chunking logic, or severity architecture changed; no signal IDs added or removed; only pattern coverage and field-eligibility narrowed/extended on already-registered signals (global-dob, meta.author, meta.confidential, meta.department, meta.ma_deal, meta.hr_employee, meta.legal, meta.financial, meta.ip). AG-369/370/371/374/376 (previously promoted) remain included unchanged: handleDrop's event.stopImmediatePropagation() Copilot drag/drop Cancel fix, the shared toRealmSafeBytes() same-realm deep-copy helper for DOCX/XLSX/PPTX Firefox Xray-wrapper extraction parity, the XLSX/PPTX extraction success-semantics fix, and the nordic-financial-terms bare-'balance' false-positive fix — all confirmed present via a byte-identical comparison of this promotion's release-built content.js/background.js against a same-session dev-built Chrome bundle (strongest available fix-presence proof; confirms the entire src/ tree, not just the two changed files, matches dev exactly). No manifest, permission, or host-scope change (permissions: ['storage'] only; host_permissions limited to the 6 listed patterns; no activeTab; no wildcard/https://*/*; no <all_urls>; connect-src 'none'). No telemetry, network diagnostics, or centralized logging added; no raw document content, file names, or personal data logged or persisted (production bundle strips all console.log/console.debug via terser pure_funcs, verified; zero new diagnostic calls in either changed file). The local, real-world reproduction files referenced during AG-376's diagnosis (docs/test-files/1.txt, 2.txt, 3.txt) were never committed, never quoted in any report, and are not part of this or any prior promotion; AG-378/379 used only synthetic fixtures. Dev-side verification (this promotion): tsc 0 errors; AG-378 PDF Author metadata suite 15/15; AG-379 metadata confidential field-awareness suite 19/19; CORE 55/55; corpus gold harness 30/0/1-blocked (pre-existing, unrelated); clipboard gold harness 125/125; signal-manifest-integrity 24/24 (no registration drift, 93 pack pattern IDs / 101 manifest constants unchanged); verify-manifest 28/28; Chrome build 34/34 + 20/20 consumer-copy; Firefox build 25/25. Release-side verification: release-verify.ts 10/10 gates (type-check, build:chrome + artifact/console-strip checks, package:chrome + no-maps/no-TS guards, license UX guardrail, no-activity-record, message-handler safety, storage manager, storage hardening, signal-manifest integrity, manifest verification); Chrome build artifact checks 31/31 + 20/20 consumer-copy guard; ZIP contents confirmed as exactly the 14 expected production files (no .map/.ts/reports/dev URLs/prompt IDs). No store upload; no GitHub Release; no website repo modified.
